Suggestion for Mercury Language: Improved Error Handling,"Hi Aetheros Support Team,
I'm writing to suggest an improvement for the Mercury language. While I find it generally very powerful for API development, the error handling could be more robust.

Specifically, it would be incredibly helpful if error messages could provide more context about the location and cause of the error. For example, including the line number and a more detailed explanation within the error message itself would significantly speed up debugging.
Thank you for considering this suggestion. I believe it would make a big difference in the developer experience.

[UAI] CfP: DATE05: Subtrack Real-time Systems,"Call for Papers Subtrack on Real-Time systems - DATE 05 http://www.idt.mdh.se/DATE05RT/cfp The 8th DATE conference and exhibition is the main European event bringing together design automation researchers, users and vendors, as well as specialists in the design, test, and manufacturing of electronic systems and circuits. DATE is also Europe's leading commercial exhibition showing the state-of-the-art in design and test tools, methodologies, IP and design services. Both the conference and the exhibition, together with the many user group meetings, fringe meetings, university booth and social events offer a wide variety of opportunities to meet and exchange information. SUBTRACK ON REAL-TIME SYSTEMS ============================= This year, DATE features a subtrack on real-time systems, to provide a forum to bring together embedded and real-time systems researchers and practitioners. Submissions of papers describing original research in all areas of real-time computing and communication systems including design, analysis, implementation, testing, and tools are invited for consideration towards presentation and publication. Chair: Gerhard Fohler, Malardalen University, Sweden Co Chair: Sanjoy Baruah, University of North Carolina, USA Technical Program Committee: Alan Burns, University of York, UK Giorgio Buttazzo, University of Pavia, Italy Jean-Dominique Decotignie, CSEM, Switzerland Peter Puschner, Vienna University of Technology, Austria Tei Wei Kuo, National Taiwan University, TAIWAN, ROC Important Dates: Submission deadline: Sep 12, 2004 (firm) Notification of acceptance: Nov 12, 2004 Camera-ready papers due: Dec 04, 2004 Submission Instructions Please visit the DATE 05 site at http://www.date-conference.com for further information about the conference.",0,0

Mercury Language - Missing Feature: Async/Await,"Hi Aetheros Support Team,

I'm writing to suggest a new feature for the Mercury language. I believe adding support for async/await functionality would greatly improve the language's ability to handle asynchronous operations in a more readable and maintainable way.

Many modern languages offer this feature, and it has become a standard for writing asynchronous code. Implementing async/await in Mercury would make it easier for developers to write efficient and performant APIs, especially when dealing with I/O-bound operations.
I believe this addition would make Mercury even more powerful and attractive to developers. I look forward to your thoughts on this suggestion.

I am writing to inform you about a critical issue that has led to substantial delays in our project. Several integrations failed concurrently, and we have not been able to address the problem despite our efforts.

The issue began after a recent update, suggesting that there might be conflicts or API changes causing the integrations to fail. We have tried restarting the services, reviewing logs, and verifying settings, but to no avail.

We would greatly appreciate your urgent attention to this matter and would be thankful for any advice or a solution you can provide. Please inform us if there are any additional steps we can take or if there is any further information you need from us.
